title: Method of obtaining a graded pecvd layer and plasma source assembly
abstract: Plasma source assembly (1) within a plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition (PECVD) device and method of providing a flow of particles to a substrate. A plasma treatment outlet of a housing (8) forms a plasma process zone for depositing a layer on the surface (2) by relative movement of the surface (2) along the plasma treatment outlet. The housing (8) hasan upstream elongated edge (6) and a downstream elongated edge (7). A plasma creation zone (3) is present within the housing (8), as well as one or more input ports (9-11) positioned in the plasma process zone for providing a process gas to the plasma creation zone (3). The plasma source assembly (1) is further arranged to provide a gradient in the concentration of particles in the flow as measured from the upstream elongated edge(6) to the downstream elongated edge (7).
claims: Claims 1 . Method of providing a flow of particles to a substrate, comprising the steps of providing a plasma source assembly (1 ) of a plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition (PECVD) device over a surface (2) of a substrate (12) to be treated for depositing a layer onto the surface (2), the plasma source assembly (1) comprising a housing (8) having a plasma treatment outlet which in operation is close to the surface (2) and forms a plasma process zone for depositing a layer on the surface (2) by relative movement of the surface (2) along the plasma treatment outlet, the housing (8) having an upstream elongated edge (6) and a downstream elongated edge (7) extending substantially perpendicular to the relative movement of the surface (2); and one or more input ports (9-1 1) positioned in the plasma process zone for providing a process gas to a plasma creation zone (3) within the housing (8); the method further comprising providing a gradient in the concentration in one or more types of particles in the flow as measured from the upstream elongated edge (6) to the downstream elongated edge (7) of the plasma source assembly (1). 2. Method according to claim 1 , further comprising the step of adding an additional process gas using an additional input port (15) positioned upstream from the upstream elongated edge (6). 3. Method according to claim 1 or 2, further comprising the step of adding an additional process gas using an additional input port (15) positioned downstream from the downstream elongated edge (7). 4. Method according to claim 1 , 2 or 3, further comprising the step of adding an additional flow of particles originating from an upstream plasma source assembly (1 '). 5. Method according to any one of claims 1 -4, further comprising the step of adding an additional flow of particles originating from a downstream plasma source assembly (1 '). 6. Method according to any one of claims 1 -5, wherein the one or more input ports comprise a first process gas port (9) remote from the plasma treatment outlet, and one or more second process gas ports (10, 1 1) positioned near to the upstream and/or downstream elongated edge (6, 7), respectively. 7. Method according to claim 6, wherein a volume flow to the upstream positioned second process gas port (10) is different from a volume flow to the downstream positioned second process gas port (1 1). 8. Method according to claim 6 or 7, wherein the upstream positioned second process gas port (10) is positioned in the plasma process zone closer to or further from the plasma creation zone (3) than the downstream positioned second process gas port (1 1 ). 9. Method according to any one of claims 1 -8, further comprising removing particles close to the upstream or downstream elongated edge (6, 7) upstream or downstream therefrom by means of a suction pump (16). 10. Method according to any one of claims 1 -9, wherein the plasma creation zone (3) is shaped to provide a higher or lower concentration of particles close to the upstream elongated edge (6) as compared to close to the downstream elongated edge (7). 1 1 . Method according to any one of claims 1 -10, wherein the plasma creation zone (3) is positioned asymmetrically to the upstream elongated edge (6) and the downstream elongated edge (7). 12. Plasma source assembly for a plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition (PECVD) device, comprising a housing (8) having a plasma treatment outlet which in operation is close to a surface (2) of a substrate (12) to be treated and forms a plasma process zone for depositing a layer on the surface (2) by relative movement of the surface (2) along the plasma treatment outlet, the housing (8) having an upstream elongated edge (6) and a downstream elongated edge (7); a plasma creation zone (3) positioned within the housing (8); one or more input ports (9-1 1) positioned in the plasma process zone for providing a process gas to the plasma creation zone (3); wherein the plasma source assembly (1 ) is further arranged to provide a gradient in the concentration of particles in the flow as measured from the upstream elongated edge (6) to the downstream elongated edge (7). 13. Plasma source assembly (1) according to claim 12, further comprising an additional input port (15) positioned upstream from the upstream elongated edge (6). 14. Plasma source assembly (1) according to claim 12, further comprising an additional input port (15) positioned downstream from the downstream elongated edge (7). 15. Plasma source assembly (1) according to any one of the claims 12-14, further comprising an upstream plasma source assembly (1 ') positioned upstream from the upstream elongated edge (6) for providing an additional inflow of particles. 16. Plasma source assembly (1) according to any one of the claims 12-15, further comprising a downstream plasma source assembly (1 ') positioned downstream from the downstream elongated edge (7) for providing an additional inflow of particles. 17. Plasma source assembly (1) according to any one of claims 12-16, wherein the one or more input ports (9-1 1) comprise a first process gas port (9) remote from the plasma treatment outlet, and one or more second process gas ports (10, 1 1) positioned near to the upstream and downstream elongated edge (6, 7), respectively. 18. Plasma source assembly (1) according to claim 17, further comprising an input gas flow control device arranged to control a volume flow to the upstream positioned second process gas port (10) which is different from a volume flow to the downstream positioned second process gas port (1 1 ). 19. Plasma source assembly (1) according to claim 17 or 18, wherein the upstream positioned second process gas port (10) is provided in the plasma process zone closer to or further from the plasma creation zone (3) than the downstream positioned second process gas port (1 1). 20. Plasma source assembly (1) according to any one of claims 12-19, further comprising a suction pump (16) positioned upstream or downstream of the upstream or downstream elongated edge (6, 7). 21 . Plasma source assembly (1) according to any one of claims 12-20, wherein the plasma creation zone (3) is shaped to provide a higher or lower concentration of particles close to the upstream elongated edge (6) as compared to close to the downstream elongated edge (7). 22. Plasma source assembly (1) according to any one of claims 12-21 , wherein the plasma creation zone (3) is positioned asymmetrically to the upstream elongated edge (6) and downstream elongated edge (7). 23. Photovoltaic module comprising a plurality of photovoltaic cells, each photovoltaic cell comprising a gradient layer deposited by the method according to any one of claims 1 -1 1 or using a plasma source assembly (1) in a PECVD device according to any one of claims 12-22.
